question: According to the docs, what is the recommended overall approach to testing executable BPMN processes?

options.text: Write fast automated unit tests against a localized, isolated workflow engine, and verify with integration tests in an environment that mirrors production before release.
Skip automated tests for the process model itself — process behavior can only be meaningfully validated by domain experts in production.
Run only manual exploratory tests directly against the production environment to keep tests in sync with real-world data.
Test BPMN processes purely with end-to-end UI tests, because the workflow engine cannot be isolated in a unit test.

a.text: The docs open with this guidance directly: test executable BPMN processes as you would any software, write fast automated unit tests with a localized and isolated workflow engine, and verify with integration tests in an environment that closely mirrors production before releasing.

b.text: The docs explicitly endorse automated tests; expert-only validation in production is not the recommended baseline.

c.text: Manual exploratory testing in production is mentioned as a complement to integration testing, not as a substitute for fast automated tests.

d.text: Camunda 8.8 Process Test makes it straightforward to embed an isolated engine in a unit test, so end-to-end UI tests are not the only option.

explanation: The docs frame process testing as ordinary software testing: prefer fast automated tests against an isolated workflow engine for everyday feedback, and use integration tests in a production-like environment — including human-driven exploratory tests — before a release.
